title: How to get set up for wallet work day (FWEB3 class primer)
html:
TL;DR

  • Saved class-primer doc walking students through MetaMask wallet setup, Polygon/Matic network configuration, and acquiring FWEB3 + MATIC tokens before a hands-on workshop
  • Step-by-step: install MetaMask Chrome extension, secure 12-word recovery phrase (write physically, never screenshot, never share), pin to browser, switch network from Ethereum to Polygon via polygonscan.com
  • The FWEB3 creator pre-funded the class — students put their wallet address into a shared Google Sheet rather than using the public faucet
  • Likely Corvallis Crypto Club / OSIG-adjacent workshop material from June 2023; voice ("I cannot overstate the importance of this phrase") is teacher-not-Yahir, so this is a saved resource, not authored
  • Companion to 2022 03 08 web3 starter pack — that doc is the conceptual primer, this one is the operational on-ramp

Notes

Like the 2022 03 08 web3 starter pack, this is a saved third-party resource that ended up in Yahir's Google Drive, not Yahir-authored content. The voice is unmistakably an instructor walking a class through wallet setup before a hands-on session.

What it tells the vault:

  • Yahir was actively in FWEB3's orbit by June 2023. FWEB3 was the gamified Polygon-onboarding project that ran in 2022–2023; a class needing FWEB3 tokens implies active community participation.
  • The teaching is security-paranoid in the right ways — no screenshots of seed phrase, paper-only backup, password-manager-OK for the MetaMask password but not the 12-word phrase. This is the canonical custody hygiene Yahir would have absorbed early.
  • Polygon-not-Ethereum is the default for hands-on — gas-cost reality of mid-2023. Compare with the conceptual Ethereum-first framing in 2022 03 08 web3 starter pack.

This is a reference bookmark more than a thinking artifact, but it dates Yahir's continuous Web3 engagement from the Corvallis Crypto Club / OSIG era through mid-2023, bridging into the LLM-pivot phase.

related.label: Web3 Starter Pack (saved primer by Jay Drain Jr.)
